children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a voluntary public access program under which States and tribal governments may apply for funding to encourage owners and operators of privately-held farm, ranch, and forest land to voluntarily make that land available for access by the public for wildlife-dependent recreation, including hunting or fishing under programs administered by the States and tribal governments.

children.label: B
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, other recreational purposes; and
the methods that will be used to achieve those benefits.
to maximize participation by offering a program the terms of which are likely to meet with widespread acceptance among landowners;
to ensure that land enrolled under the State or tribal government program has appropriate wildlife habitat;

to strengthen wildlife habitat improvement efforts on land enrolled in a conservation reserve enhancement program under or on land covered by a wetland reserve easement under by providing incentives to increase public hunting and other recreational access on that land;section 3831a of this titlesection 3865c of this title
to use additional Federal, State, tribal government, or private resources in carrying out the program; and
to make available to the public the location of land enrolled.

children.heading: No preemption
children.label: 1 No preemption
children.text: Nothing in this section preempts a State or tribal government law, including any State or tribal government liability law.

children.heading: Effect of inconsistent opening dates for migratory bird hunting
children.label: 2 Effect of inconsistent opening dates for migratory bird hunting
children.text: The Secretary shall reduce by 25 percent the amount of funding otherwise determined for a State under the program if the opening dates for migratory bird hunting in the State are not consistent for residents and non-residents.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: e Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall promulgate such regulations as are necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Mandatory funding
children.label: 1 Mandatory funding
children.text: Of the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall use to carry out this section, to the maximum extent practicable, $50,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2009 through 2012, $40,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2014 through 2018, $50,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, $10,000,000 for fiscal year 2024, and $70,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2031.

children.heading: Enhanced public access to wetland reserve easements
children.label: 2 Enhanced public access to wetland reserve easements
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, of the funds made available under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall use $3,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2019 through 2023 to encourage public access to land covered by wetland reserve easements under through agreements with States and tribal governments under this section.section 3865c of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $10,000,000 for fiscal year 2013.
